places.name: DeploymentReady
IncidentBeingInvestigated
DeploymentInProgress
CompletedDeployments
ResolvedIncidents

transitions.name: Create Deployment
Incident Raised
Start Deployment
transitions.lambdaCode:
/**
 * Safety-gated deployment start.
 *
 * The boolean logic is intentionally simple: if the transition is enabled by
 * the Petri-net arcs, it may fire. The important behaviour is in the arcs:
 * - DeploymentReady is a standard input arc, so one queued deployment is consumed.
 * - IncidentBeingInvestigated is an inhibitor arc, so any active incident blocks firing.
 * - DeploymentInProgress is an inhibitor arc, so only one deployment may run at a time.
 */
export default Lambda(() => true);

transitions.name: Finish Deployment
Close Incident
Deployment Causes Incident

differentialEquations.name: Deployment Age
Incident Age

metrics.name: Successful deployments
metrics.description: Cumulative number of deployments that finished without causing an incident.

metrics.name: Failed deployments
metrics.description: Cumulative number of deployments that rolled back and opened an incident.

metrics.name: Release queue length
metrics.description: How many deployments are waiting behind the safety gate.

metrics.name: Active incidents
metrics.description: Number of incidents currently blocking the deployment gate.

metrics.name: Deployment gate blocked
metrics.description: Returns 1 when an incident or active deployment is preventing another deployment from starting; otherwise 0.

metrics.name: Failure share
metrics.description: Share of completed-or-failed deployments that ended in failure.

scenarios.name: Baseline operations
scenarios.description: Balanced deployment rate, moderate risk, and normal incident flow. Good starting point for explaining the inhibitor gate.

scenarios.name: Incident surge
scenarios.description: External incidents arrive quickly and block the deployment gate, causing the release queue to pile up.

scenarios.name: High deployment velocity
scenarios.description: A high release creation rate tests whether the single-deployment safety gate becomes the bottleneck.

scenarios.name: Risky large releases
scenarios.description: Larger, riskier releases take longer and more often create incidents, visibly closing the inhibitor gate.
